seo_title: Current Time in Las Vegas — PST/PDT
seo_description: Current local time in Las Vegas, Nevada. Pacific Time (PST UTC-8 / PDT UTC-7). No Arizona exception — Nevada observes DST.

faq.question: What time is it in Las Vegas right now?
faq.answer: Las Vegas, Nevada, USA uses Pacific Standard Time (PST, UTC-8) in winter and Pacific Daylight Time (PDT, UTC-7) in summer. Clocks spring forward on the second Sunday of March and fall back on the first Sunday of November — the same schedule as California and the entire US West Coast. The IANA identifier is America/Los_Angeles.

faq.question: What time zone is Las Vegas in?
faq.answer: Las Vegas is in the Pacific Time Zone, sharing PST/PDT with Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Seattle. During standard time (November–March), Las Vegas uses PST (UTC-8). During daylight saving time (March–November), it uses PDT (UTC-7) — 3 hours behind New York and 8 hours behind London. The IANA identifier is America/Los_Angeles.

faq.question: Does Las Vegas observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Las Vegas (Nevada) observes daylight saving time. Clocks spring forward 1 hour to PDT (UTC-7) on the second Sunday of March at 2:00 AM, and fall back to PST (UTC-8) on the first Sunday of November at 2:00 AM. Nevada is unlike neighboring Arizona, which does not observe DST — meaning Las Vegas and Phoenix share the same time in summer but differ by 1 hour in winter.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Las Vegas and New York?
faq.answer: Las Vegas (PST/PDT) is exactly 3 hours behind New York (EST/EDT) year-round. Both cities observe daylight saving time on the same schedule, so the 3-hour gap never changes. When it is 12:00 PM (noon) in New York, it is 9:00 AM in Las Vegas.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Las Vegas and London?
faq.answer: Las Vegas is 8 hours behind London for most of the year: PST (UTC-8) vs GMT (UTC+0) = 8 hours, and PDT (UTC-7) vs BST (UTC+1) = 8 hours. Brief 7-hour windows occur in spring (when the US switches to PDT before the UK switches to BST) and in autumn (when the UK falls back before the US). When it is 6:00 PM in London (GMT), it is 10:00 AM in Las Vegas.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Las Vegas and Los Angeles?
faq.answer: There is zero time difference between Las Vegas and Los Angeles — both are in the Pacific Time Zone (PST/PDT) and change clocks on identical schedules. Las Vegas and Los Angeles always show exactly the same time.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Las Vegas from the US?
faq.answer: From New York (EST), call Las Vegas between 12:00 PM and 6:00 PM Eastern — that is 9:00 AM–3:00 PM Pacific, catching full Las Vegas business hours. From Chicago (CST), call between 11:00 AM and 5:00 PM. Casino lines operate 24/7; for hotel reservations and show bookings, aim for 9:00 AM–5:00 PM Pacific.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Las Vegas?
faq.answer: Standard Las Vegas office hours are 9:00 AM–5:00 PM PST/PDT, Monday through Friday. Banks operate 9:00 AM–5:00 PM weekdays. Las Vegas is famously a 24-hour city: major casino resorts (Bellagio, MGM Grand, Caesars Palace) never close. The Strip's retail runs 10:00 AM–11:00 PM and entertainment shows run 7:00 PM–11:00 PM, with nightclubs open from 10:00 PM to 4:00 AM.

timezone_facts.dst_end: First Sunday in November at 2:00 AM
